perturbation.original_text: Each capitalized term used but not defined herein shall have the meaning assigned in the SLCMA.
perturbation.changed_text: Each capitalized term used but not defined herein shall have the meaning assigned in the SLCMA, except for 'Software,' which shall refer to only the components explicitly listed in Addendum A.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that all capitalized terms not defined in the Schedule will take their meaning from the SLCMA, but then creates an exception for the term 'Software.' This means that if the SLCMA has a broader definition of 'Software,' the Schedule limits it, creating ambiguity. In the following text, this will now create In-Text contradiction when referering to the term `Software` as a capitalized term, and now has a special and narrow definition that breaks the agreement that the SLCMA's definition shall be used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each capitalized term used but not defined herein shall have the meaning assigned in the SLCMA, except for 'Software,' which shall refer to only the components explicitly listed in Addendum A. WHEREAS, Bank of America and Supplier entered into the SLCMA in order to set forth the terms and conditions pursuant to which Supplier provides certain Software to Bank of America, WHEREAS, the parties desire to add to the SLCMA the Supplier Offer Placement System Software; NOW THEREFORE, in consideration of the promises and accords made herein, and the exchange of such good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which is hereby acknowledged, Bank of America and Supplier agree as follows: The attached Schedule [A] is hereby incorporated into the SLCMA describing the Offer Placement System Software for use by Bank of America.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. The original text states that undefined capitalized terms take their meaning from the SLCMA. The changed text introduces an exception for 'Software,' limiting its definition to Addendum A, which contradicts the initial agreement of defaulting to the SLCMA definition, creating ambiguity and potential unenforceability because 'Software' is defined differently based on the context, which would likely cause confusion. This aligns with the definition of "Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction: Ambiguities occur when key terms or obligations are defined inconsistently in different parts of a contract, making enforcement difficult or interpretation unclear."
